production_metadata.audio_contract: cached AI audio first with browser speech fallback; all audio fields optional

subject: English
skill: Reading Fluency and Expression With Complex Text

lesson.story_mission: Enter Fluency Theater, where Grade 5 readers rehearse complex sentences and passages so accuracy, rate, phrasing, punctuation, expression, and meaning work together.
lesson.objective: I can read Grade 5 prose and informational text with accuracy, appropriate rate, phrasing, punctuation, and expression.
lesson.concept_explanation: Fluent readers do not simply read quickly. They preserve exact words, group words into meaningful phrases, slow down for complex ideas, use punctuation as voice directions, and reread to make meaning clearer.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may skip small words, break phrases in awkward places, ignore punctuation, or use a flat voice that hides the author’s meaning.
lesson.real_world_connection: Fluency helps when reading novels, articles, primary sources, science explanations, speeches, and directions aloud or silently.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read accurately, use a clear rate, phrase ideas smoothly, follow punctuation, reread with purpose, and match expression to meaning.
lesson.mini_lesson: First read for exact words. Then mark phrase groups and punctuation. Finally reread with a voice that fits the text and supports comprehension.

audio.text: accuracy, rate, phrasing, punctuation, expression, repeated reading, meaning
audio.label: Listen
audio.student_prompt: Listen, then say the fluency words aloud.

worked_examples.prompt: Which word must be read exactly as printed in the sentence?
hints: First, read every word exactly as printed.
Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.
Finally, reread with a voice that matches the meaning of the text.
worked_examples.explanation: The printed word is delicate, so accurate reading keeps that exact word.
worked_examples.feedback: Strong fluent reading choice. You used the words, phrasing, punctuation, or meaning in the text.

question_audio.text: Which word must be read exactly as printed in the sentence? Choices: delicate, distant, difficult.
question_audio.label: Read Question

worked_examples.prompt: Choose the best phrase grouping for smooth reading.

hints: First, read every word exactly as printed.
Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.
Finally, reread with a voice that matches the meaning of the text.
worked_examples.explanation: The best grouping keeps subject, action, and time phrase together.
worked_examples.feedback: Strong fluent reading choice. You used the words, phrasing, punctuation, or meaning in the text.

question_audio.text: Choose the best phrase grouping for smooth reading. Choices: The young inventor / tested her design / before the science fair., The young / inventor tested / her design before / the science fair., The young inventor tested / her / design before the / science fair..

checkpoint.prompt: After a first read with two skipped words, what should the reader do next?
checkpoint.correct_answer: reread to fix accuracy before adding expression
hints: First, read every word exactly as printed.
Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.
Finally, reread with a voice that matches the meaning of the text.
checkpoint.explanation: Repeated reading begins by correcting missed words so meaning is accurate.
checkpoint.feedback: Strong fluent reading choice. You used the words, phrasing, punctuation, or meaning in the text.

question_audio.text: After a first read with two skipped words, what should the reader do next? Choices: reread to fix accuracy before adding expression, read faster to finish, ignore the skipped words.

skips_words.description: The reader omits, substitutes, or changes printed words.
skips_words.feedback: Point to each word and reread the sentence exactly as written before improving speed or expression.

phrase_chunking_error.description: The reader breaks a sentence in places that interrupt meaning or rate.
phrase_chunking_error.feedback: Find who or what the sentence is about, then group words that tell one idea together.
phrase_chunking_error.remediation: Return to Level 2: Phrasing and Rate.

punctuation_ignored.description: The reader reads through commas, periods, question marks, or other punctuation without useful pauses or voice changes.
punctuation_ignored.feedback: Treat punctuation as directions for pausing, stopping, or changing your voice.
punctuation_ignored.remediation: Return to Level 3: Punctuation and Expression.

expression_flat_reading.description: The reader uses the same flat tone even when the text shows feeling, dialogue, suspense, or importance.
expression_flat_reading.feedback: Ask what the words mean and how the speaker or narrator might sound, then reread with matching expression.

review_bank.prompt: Which rereading plan best supports meaning?
review_bank.correct_answer: read once for words, again for phrases, and again for expression
hints: First, read every word exactly as printed.
Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.
Finally, reread with a voice that matches the meaning of the text.
review_bank.explanation: A purposeful repeated-reading plan builds accuracy, phrasing, and expression.
review_bank.feedback: Strong fluent reading choice. You used the words, phrasing, punctuation, or meaning in the text.

question_audio.text: Which rereading plan best supports meaning? Choices: read once for words, again for phrases, and again for expression, read once as fast as possible, read only the first and last sentence.

game_theme.title: Fluency Theater: Complex Text Rehearsal
game_theme.mission_intro: Rehearse Grade 5 prose and informational text until every word, phrase, pause, and tone helps the audience understand.
game_theme.unlock_message: You can now rehearse complex Grade 5 passages in the Skill Practice Center.

question_audio.text: Which evidence shows fluency improved after rereading? Choices: The reader kept all words accurate and sounded more like the passage felt., The reader skipped longer words but finished sooner., The reader read louder without noticing punctuation..
question_audio.label: Read Question

level_banks.label: Mixed
level_banks.focus: Mixed fluency accuracy, phrasing, punctuation, expression, and repeated reading

questions.prompt: Which small word changes the meaning if it is skipped?
hints: First, read every word exactly as printed.
Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.
Finally, reread with a voice that matches the meaning of the text.
questions.explanation: Skipping not reverses the meaning, so it must be read accurately.
questions.feedback: Strong fluent reading choice. You used the words, phrasing, punctuation, or meaning in the text.

question_audio.text: Which small word changes the meaning if it is skipped? Choices: not, the, river.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: Choose the best phrase grouping for smooth reading.
questions.correct_answer: The young inventor / tested her design / before the science fair.
hints: First, read every word exactly as printed.
Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.
Finally, reread with a voice that matches the meaning of the text.
questions.explanation: The best grouping keeps subject, action, and time phrase together.
questions.feedback: Strong fluent reading choice. You used the words, phrasing, punctuation, or meaning in the text.

question_audio.text: Choose the best phrase grouping for smooth reading. Choices: The young inventor / tested her design / before the science fair., The young / inventor tested / her design before / the science fair., The young inventor tested / her / design before the / science fair..
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.prompt: How should your voice change at the question mark?
questions.correct_answer: rise slightly to show a question
hints: First, read every word exactly as printed.
Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.
Finally, reread with a voice that matches the meaning of the text.
questions.explanation: A question mark signals a questioning voice, often with a slight rise.
questions.feedback: Strong fluent reading choice. You used the words, phrasing, punctuation, or meaning in the text.

question_audio.text: How should your voice change at the question mark? Choices: rise slightly to show a question, stay completely flat, speed up and ignore the mark.
question_audio.label: Read Question

speed up and ignore the mark

questions.prompt: After a first read with two skipped words, what should the reader do next?
questions.correct_answer: reread to fix accuracy before adding expression
hints: First, read every word exactly as printed.
Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.
Finally, reread with a voice that matches the meaning of the text.
questions.explanation: Repeated reading begins by correcting missed words so meaning is accurate.
questions.feedback: Strong fluent reading choice. You used the words, phrasing, punctuation, or meaning in the text.

question_audio.text: After a first read with two skipped words, what should the reader do next? Choices: reread to fix accuracy before adding expression, read faster to finish, ignore the skipped words.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.text: First read: The canal carried water to nearby farms. Reread: The narrow canal carried water to nearby farms during dry months.

Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.

questions.explanation: The tool named in the sentence is microscope.

questions.prompt: Which phrase should be kept together to show cause?
hints: First, read every word exactly as printed.
Next, group words that belong together and notice punctuation marks.
Finally, reread with a voice that matches the meaning of the text.
questions.explanation: The opening cause phrase belongs together before the pause.
questions.feedback: Strong fluent reading choice. You used the words, phrasing, punctuation, or meaning in the text.

questions.explanation: Brief pauses at phrase breaks make a complex sentence easier to understand.

story.text: Read This Page. Welcome to the Grade 5 Fluency Theater for complex text. You will learn to read every word accurately, choose a useful rate, follow punctuation, and make your voice match meaning. Press Start Mission when you are ready to rehearse like a thoughtful reader.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: Read This Page. This mini lesson teaches fluent reading with complex prose and informational text. First, read accurately so no small word changes meaning. Next, group words into phrases and use punctuation as traffic signs for your voice. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Read This Page. On this Watch page, listen to a model reader think aloud. Notice how the reader checks exact words, pauses at commas, and changes expression when a sentence asks a question or shows strong feeling. Press Next for the demo.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Read This Page. This demo is a safe rehearsal. Read the sentence, press Listen for a model, repeat after the model when helpful, and explain which fluency move improves understanding. Press Next when you are ready for Practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Read This Page. Practice time. Read the item, use the visual model, and press Read Question if you want the prompt read aloud. Press Listen to hear the sentence or passage before you answer.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Read This Page. Challenge time asks you to use accuracy, phrasing, punctuation, expression, and repeated reading with less help. Press Read Question for the prompt and Listen for model audio before you choose or write your answer.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Read This Page. Checkpoint time shows what you can do independently. Press Read Question if you want narration, use Listen for sentences and passages, and check that your answer matches the text and fluency goal.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Read This Page. This badge celebrates your Grade 5 fluency work. You practiced reading complex sentences and passages with accurate words, smooth phrasing, punctuation pauses, expression, and meaningful rereading. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Read This Page. Your profile saves stars, accuracy, hints, and level progress. If one level needs more work, choose Practice This Skill and return to the Skill Practice Center. Fluent readers keep rehearsing with purpose.
profile.label: Read This Page
